properties.name: rqueue.retry.max
properties.description: Global retry limit used when a listener does not configure numRetries and would otherwise retry forever. -1 preserves the retry-forever default. Explicit listener retry counts and DLQ defaults take precedence.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.auto-create-streams
properties.description: When true (default), Rqueue creates JetStream streams for each registered queue at startup via NatsStreamValidator. Set to false for locked-down JetStream accounts where the application credentials lack add_stream permission; NatsStreamValidator will then verify that every required stream already exists and abort boot with a clear error listing any missing ones.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.auto-create-consumers
properties.description: When true (default), Rqueue lazily creates durable pull consumers on the first pop call for each (stream, consumerName) pair and caches the subscription in-process. Set to false to fail-fast on missing consumers instead.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.auto-create-dlq-stream
properties.description: When true, Rqueue automatically creates a dead-letter queue stream for each registered queue that declares a deadLetterQueue in its @RqueueListener. Default is false.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.auto-create-kv-buckets
properties.description: When true (default), each NATS-backed store lazily creates its KV bucket on first use. Set to false for locked-down JetStream accounts; NatsKvBucketValidator will verify at startup that every required bucket already exists and abort boot listing any missing ones. Pre-create the buckets with: nats kv add rqueue-queue-config / rqueue-jobs / rqueue-locks / rqueue-message-metadata / rqueue-workers / rqueue-worker-heartbeats.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.url
properties.description: NATS server URL (e.g. nats://localhost:4222). Used when a single server URL is sufficient. Use rqueue.nats.connection.urls for a cluster.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.credentials-path
properties.description: Path to a NATS credentials file (.creds) for NKey/JWT-based authentication.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.username
properties.description: Username for NATS username/password authentication.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.password
properties.description: Password for NATS username/password authentication.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.token
properties.description: Token for NATS token-based authentication.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.tls
properties.description: Enable TLS for the NATS connection.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.connection-name
properties.description: Logical name for this NATS connection, visible in server monitoring.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.connect-timeout
properties.description: Maximum time to wait for the initial connection to the NATS server.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.reconnect-wait
properties.description: Time to wait between reconnect attempts when the NATS connection is lost.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.max-reconnects
properties.description: Maximum number of reconnect attempts. -1 means unlimited.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.connection.ping-interval
properties.description: Interval between server pings to detect a stale connection.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.stream.replicas
properties.description: Number of stream replicas. Must not exceed the number of JetStream-enabled servers in the cluster.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.stream.storage
properties.description: Storage backend for streams: FILE (default, durable) or MEMORY (faster, lost on restart).

properties.name: rqueue.nats.stream.retention
properties.description: Stream retention policy: LIMITS (default, bounded by max-age/max-bytes/max-messages), INTEREST (remove when no consumers), or WORK_QUEUE (remove on ack).

properties.name: rqueue.nats.stream.max-age
properties.description: Maximum age of messages in the stream before they are discarded. Default is 14 days.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.stream.max-bytes
properties.description: Maximum total size in bytes of all messages in the stream. -1 means unlimited.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.stream.max-messages
properties.description: Maximum number of messages in the stream. -1 means unlimited.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.stream.discard-policy
properties.description: Policy for discarding messages when stream limits are reached: OLD (default, discard oldest) or NEW (reject new messages).

properties.name: rqueue.nats.stream.duplicate-window
properties.description: Deduplication window for the Nats-Msg-Id header. Messages with the same ID published within this window are deduplicated by the server. Default is 2 minutes.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.consumer.ack-wait
properties.description: Time the server waits for an ack before redelivering the message. Should be longer than the slowest expected message processing time. Default is 30 seconds.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.consumer.max-deliver
properties.description: Maximum number of delivery attempts for a message before it is sent to the DLQ (if configured). Default is 3.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.consumer.max-ack-pending
properties.description: Maximum number of unacknowledged messages a consumer can hold. Default is 1000.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.consumer.fetch-wait
properties.description: Maximum time to wait for messages in a single fetch call before returning an empty result. Default is 2 seconds.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.naming.stream-prefix
properties.description: Prefix applied to every JetStream stream name created by Rqueue (e.g. rqueue-js-my-queue). Default is 'rqueue-js-'.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.naming.subject-prefix
properties.description: Prefix applied to every JetStream subject used by Rqueue (e.g. rqueue.js.my-queue). Default is 'rqueue.js.'.

properties.name: rqueue.nats.naming.dlq-suffix
properties.description: Suffix appended to the stream and subject name for dead-letter queue streams (e.g. rqueue-js-my-queue-dlq). Default is '-dlq'.

values.description: In-memory storage; faster but lost on server restart.
Retain messages subject to max-age, max-bytes, and max-messages limits (default).
Remove messages once all active consumers have acknowledged them.
Remove messages immediately after they are acknowledged by any consumer.
Discard the oldest messages when limits are reached (default).
Reject new publish calls when limits are reached.
